now that I've seen a lot of folks reviewing this film in my feed, it's probably time to share some of what i've thought.

at the preview screening, there was a significantly intimate audience. i invited two people: my boss and my best friend. both have meaningfully engaged with me in dialogue about Ari Aster films and we were all quite interested in watching this.

i generally thought it was well-made, and the consensus seemed to be unanimous among the rest of the preview audience was that Joaquin Phoenix lost himself in Sherriff Joe.

there was a moment when this movie led me to accept the fact that i had no idea what was going to happen next, and that was going to be unforgettable. i also knew that it would be divisive -- especially among the media illiterate who use movies as background for doomscrolling and then shit their opinions on the internet.

those people exist -- and that's something this movie shows us too much of. it's why i didn't give it a like, though i think it is a good movie. i feel like a second watch would give me the "yeah, I liked this" feeling just based on what i remember of the writing and the plot, but we'll see. i need to get through a number of movies before they leave theaters.
